definitions.text: Firm in standing or position; not tottering or shaking; fixed; firm.
examples: Hold the ladder steady while I go up.

definitions.text: Constant in feeling, purpose, or pursuit; not fickle, changeable, or wavering; not easily moved or persuaded to alter a purpose; resolute.
examples: a man steady in his principles, in his purpose, or in the pursuit of an object

definitions.text: Smooth and not bumpy or with obstructions.
To stabilize; to prevent from shaking.
examples: I took a drink to steady my nerves.

definitions.text: Aspect marker indicating consistency or intensity.
examples: (The addition of quotations indicative of this usage is being sought:)

definitions.text: A rest or support, as for the hand, a tool, or a piece of work.
To row with pressure at a low stroke-rating, often 18 strokes per minute.
examples: After the sprint pieces, we rowed steady for the rest of practice.
